findings: FNO2d (model_fno.py): no nn.Dropout or nn.BatchNorm layers — model.train() vs model.eval() should not change forward outputs.
train.py::_eval_loader line 91: model.eval() called before val loop.
train.py: relative_l2_loss(pred, target) used in both _eval_loader (line 98) and training loop (line 202) — identical function.
train.py: prepare_input(occ) applied in both train (line 200) and val (line 97) paths.
train.py line 194: model.train() at start of each epoch (restores after _eval_loader).
model_fno.py::relative_l2_loss (lines 130–136): per-sample ||pred-target||_2 / ||target||_2, then mean over batch. Includes ALL pixels (obstacle pixels have target=0).
model_fno.py::prepare_input (lines 121–127): concatenates occupancy with fixed [0,1] grid — no data-dependent normalization.
No running statistics or train-only normalization in train.py or model_fno.py.
Both train and val epoch metrics average per-batch relative_l2_loss values (mean of batch means). Same aggregation for both — not a train/val asymmetry.

descriptions: Sample 0 (θ=180°, idx=2687): rel-L2=0.2814. GT V range [0.000, 0.831] (free-space mean 0.404). Pred range [-0.015, 0.732] (free-space mean 0.466), pred std=0.1121, range=0.746. Prediction shows spatial variation with a plausible value-function-like structure (higher away from goal, lower near goal/obstacles) but with visible inaccuracy vs ground truth.
